context:
Section 7 (Future Research Directions), page 31, first bullet

A related question is: how well can one approximate an n-dimensional symmetric convex body with a finite intersection of m (co-centered) ellipsoids, with m growing potentially with n? Since we have shown that a GE-d, with d = 2m−3, can exactly represent an intersection of m co-centered ellipsoids (see Corollary 5.6 and Theorem 5.1), progress on this question can potentially improve our approximation factor in Theorem 5.7.
title: Best approximation of a symmetric convex body by an intersection of co-centered ellipsoids
latex:
Given an integer dimension \(n\ge 2\) and an integer \(m\ge 1\), let \(\mathcal{C}_n\) denote the family of symmetric convex bodies in \(\mathbb{R}^n\), i.e., compact convex sets \(C\subset \mathbb{R}^n\) with nonempty interior and satisfying \(C=-C\). An ellipsoid centered at the origin is a set of the form \(E(Q):=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n\mid x^\top Q x\le 1\}\), where \(Q\in \mathbb{S}^n_{++}\) is a symmetric positive definite matrix.

Define \(\alpha_{m,n}\) to be the smallest real number \(\alpha\ge 1\) such that for every \(C\in\mathcal{C}_n\) there exist \(m\) origin-centered ellipsoids \(E(Q_1),\dots,E(Q_m)\) with
\[
\bigcap_{i=1}^m E(Q_i)\subseteq C\subseteq \alpha\,\bigcap_{i=1}^m E(Q_i).
\]
Equivalently, \(\alpha_{m,n}\) is the optimal worst-case multiplicative approximation factor (in the sense of set inclusion up to scaling about the origin) achievable by intersections of \(m\) co-centered ellipsoids.

Determine \(\alpha_{m,n}\) (exactly or up to sharp asymptotic bounds) as a function of \(m\) and \(n\). In particular, characterize how large \(m=m(n)\) must be so that \(\alpha_{m,n}\le 1+\varepsilon\) for a given \(\varepsilon\in(0,1)\).

Since we have shown that a GE-d, with d = 2m−3, can exactly represent an intersection of m co-centered ellipsoids (see Corollary 5.6 and Theorem 5.1), progress on this question can potentially improve our approximation factor in Theorem 5.7.
